title: Bridge Essay: Engaging learners with games-Insights from functional near-infrared spectroscopy

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Water/Pleasure** (Phase 5) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Water/Pleasure to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Engaging learners with games-Insights from functional near-infrared spectroscopy.** — PLoS One. PMID: 37279251. DOI: 10.1371/journal.pone.0286450. The use of game elements in learning tasks is thought to facilitate emotional and behavioral responses as well as learner engagement. So far, however, little is known about the underlying neural mechanisms of game-based learning. In the current study, we added game elements to anumber line estimation task assessing fraction understanding and compared brain activation patterns to anon-game-based task version.
